title: 帷幕
summary:
Blackthorn suggests to Poirot that the killer, "X," can be identified by finding the person who knows everyone. Poirot warns him that the investigation is more complex because the guests at the inn are not strangers; the Lestrade couple, who own the inn, have invited friends and acquaintances, including the Franklins, Norton, and Miss Cole. Poirot refuses to reveal more details to protect Blackthorn from the cunning and ruthless killer.

The next morning, Blackthorn speaks with Poirot’s servant, Curtis, who reveals that Poirot’s heart disease has worsened significantly in recent months. Later, Blackthorn joins Norton, Colonel Lestrade, and Mrs. Lestrade for a game of bridge. Mrs. Lestrade plays aggressively and uses her peripheral vision to peek at opponents' cards. She also speaks harshly to her husband, making him nervous and causing him to play poorly. After the game, Norton remarks on how the Colonel has become a "pushover" under his wife's bullying.

While walking in the hall, Blackthorn notices the garden door is open. Norton informs him that Blackthorn's daughter, Judith, is outside with Alton. Disturbed by this, Blackthorn retires to his room but cannot sleep due to anxiety regarding his children's safety and his late wife's absence. Seeking sleeping pills, he encounters Alton in the dim corridor. Alton, wearing a repulsive smile, invites Blackthorn to his room to discuss his insomnia.
